commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f8c5b8428b2a6ca72c0c3c6220560f4591d61a97)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an opt-in `compat.systemPromptPlacement` setting for OpenAI Responses-compatible providers, with runtime request shaping, schema/types, docs, and targeted tests.

PR surface: Source +27, Tests +57, Docs +22. Total +106 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main places Responses system prompts into `input`, and the PR body supplies after-fix output from a strict local Responses-compatible provider that previously rejected that shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow mechanical wiring and regression coverage issues that an automated repair PR can attempt without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: [codex] support Responses system prompts in instructions
target.body:
## Summary

Add an OpenAI Responses compatibility switch for providers that reject `system` or `developer` messages inside the Responses `input` array.

When a configured Responses model sets `compat.systemPromptPlacement: "instructions"`, OpenClaw now sends `context.systemPrompt` as top-level `instructions` and keeps `input` to user/tool/replay items. The default remains unchanged: normal `openai-responses` models still place the system prompt in `input`, while Codex Responses keeps its existing top-level instructions behavior.

This covers OpenAI-compatible Responses gateways that expose the Responses API but enforce a stricter request schema and return errors such as `400 System messages are not allowed`.

## What changed

- Adds `compat.systemPromptPlacement?: "input" | "instructions"` to OpenAI Responses compat metadata.
- Wires the switch through TypeScript types, runtime model registry schema, strict config Zod schema, and request construction.
- Adds regression coverage for a local Responses-compatible provider using top-level `instructions`.
- Documents the config in the OpenAI provider proxy/compatible route notes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: A local OpenAI Responses-compatible gateway rejected OpenClaw agent turns because the generated Responses `input` array contained a system/developer message. The upstream runtime returned `400 System messages are not allowed` before this compatibility switch.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway on macOS using a configured New API-compatible provider at `http://localhost:18300/v1` with model ref `newapi-local/gpt-5.5`; this is the same setup that previously failed before reply.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a direct OpenClaw Responses transport-path request after applying the patch with `compat.systemPromptPlacement: "instructions"`, then restarted the local gateway and sent an agent request through the configured provider.

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the direct transport-path proof:

```text
{"hasInstructions":true,"roles":["user"]}
{"stopReason":"stop","text":"OK"}
```

The first line is the captured request shape after the patch: the system prompt is present as top-level `instructions`, and the Responses `input` roles contain only `user` rather than `system` or `developer`.

Observed result after fix: The provider accepted the request and returned `OK`; the local OpenClaw gateway resumed replying successfully after `openclaw gateway restart`.

What was not tested: Full upstream CI and a public hosted New API deployment were not tested locally; targeted transport/config tests, formatting, and core typecheck were run as supplemental checks.

## Verification

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check packages/llm-core/src/types.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/config/types.models.ts src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts src/llm/providers/openai-responses.ts`

## Notes

`pnpm check:changed --staged` could not run locally because the configured Crabbox/Testbox wrapper failed its binary sanity check before starting checks. The targeted local tests and core type check above passed.
target.author: skingford
